title: Garlicky Tender Uruguayan Broad Beans Gratin
description: A nostalgic Uruguayan dish featuring tender broad beans in a garlicky creamy sauce, topped with a crispy bonito shave. This recipe is Jain-friendly and perfect for a comforting dinner or lunch.

ingredients.name: dairy-free milk
ingredients.notes: such as soy or almond milk

instructions: In a large saucepan, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the garlic and onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the broad beans, chopped tomatoes, vegetable broth, and dried oregano to the saucepan. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook until the broad beans are tender, about 10-12 minutes.
Stir in the dairy-free milk and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until heated through.
Transfer the mixture to a baking dish and top with a layer of bonito flakes.
Bake in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, until the top is golden brown and the dish is heated through.
Serve hot, garnished with additional bonito flakes if desired.
